tools.name: ingest_media
tools.description: Ingest a media file (video, audio, or image) for processing. Validates the file, detects MIME type, extracts duration for video/audio, registers the asset with content-hash dedup, and enqueues an initial processing job.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local media file (video, audio, or image)

title.description: Optional human-readable title for the media asset. Defaults to the filename.

metadata.description: Optional JSON metadata to attach to the asset (e.g., pipeline config, source info)

tools.name: media_status
tools.description: Query the processing status of one or more media assets, including per-stage progress details.

asset_id.description: ID of a specific media asset to query

file_path.description: File path to look up a media asset by its original path

tools.name: extract_keyframes
tools.description: Preprocess a video asset: detect dead time, segment into windows, extract downscaled keyframes, build a subject registry, and write a pipeline manifest. Replaces the old simple keyframe extraction with a full Phase 0 preprocess pipeline.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset (must be a video)

interval_seconds.description: Interval between keyframes in seconds. Default: 1. Use 0.5 for sports/action content.

segment_duration.description: Duration of each segment window in seconds. Default: 15

dead_time_threshold.description: Sensitivity threshold for mpdecimate dead-time detection. Default: 0.02

section_config.description: Path to a JSON file with manual section boundary definitions

detect_dead_time.description: Whether to detect and skip dead time (static/idle frames). Default: false. Can be too aggressive for continuous action video like sports.

short_edge.description: Short edge resolution for downscaled frames in pixels. Default: 480

include_audio.description: Whether to extract and transcribe audio for each segment using the configured STT service. Default: false.

tools.name: analyze_keyframes
tools.description: Map video segments through Gemini's structured output API for vision-based analysis. Reads frames from the preprocess manifest, sends each segment to Gemini with assistant-provided extraction instructions and a JSON Schema for guaranteed structured output. Supports concurrency pooling, cost tracking, resumability (skips segments with existing results), and automatic retries with exponential backoff.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset whose segments to analyze

mode.description: Analysis mode: 'keyframes' extracts frames and sends images (default), 'direct_video' uploads video directly to Gemini. Direct video lets Gemini see motion/temporal context but has a 2GB file size limit.

system_prompt.description: Assistant-provided extraction instructions for Gemini (e.g., what to look for in the frames)

output_schema.description: JSON Schema for structured output - Gemini will enforce this schema on the response

context.description: Additional context to include in the prompt (e.g., subject registry, domain-specific info)

concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent Gemini API requests. Default: 10

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts per segment on failure. Default: 3

tools.name: query_media
tools.description: Query video analysis data using natural language. Sends map output (from analyze_keyframes) to Claude for intelligent analysis and Q&A. Supports arbitrary questions about video content - Claude reads the full structured analysis and answers based on the data.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset to query

query.description: Natural language query about the video data (e.g., 'What happens at the 5 minute mark?', 'Summarize the key events', 'Are there any scoring plays?')

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt for Claude to customize analysis behavior

model.description: LLM model to use for analysis. Default: 'claude-sonnet-4-6'

tools.name: generate_clip
tools.description: Extract a video clip from a media asset using ffmpeg. Applies configurable pre/post-roll padding (clamped to file boundaries), outputs the clip as a temporary file, and registers it as an attachment for in-chat delivery.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset (must be a video)

start_time.description: Start time of the clip in seconds

end_time.description: End time of the clip in seconds

pre_roll.description: Seconds of padding before start_time. Default: 3

post_roll.description: Seconds of padding after end_time. Default: 2

title.description: Short descriptive title for the clip (e.g. 'snow-dive-closeup', 'goal-celebration'). Used as the filename. If omitted, falls back to timestamp-based naming.
